entry.title: Re: Time Dependency in SAP Business Partner: Maintenance and Data Distribution to Non-Time-Dependent
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

In SAP Release 7.0, time dependency was significantly enhanced to provide a more comprehensive and consistent approach to data management in SAP Business Partner. 

Full time dependency was introduced for central data through table BUT000_TD, ensuring that even core Business Partner attributes could be maintained with validity periods. 

Additionally, EEW (Enhancement Framework) extensions were also enabled with time dependency, allowing custom fields to follow the same time-based behavior. 

With these enhancements, SAP achieved complete time-sliced Business Partner data management, where all relevant data can be maintained, tracked, and processed based on validity periods across the entire Business Partner model. 

"

 

Thanks for your kindly sharing again, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Max Success Plan von SAP - die Strategische Partnerschaft auf höchstem Niveau
entry.content:
Im dritten und letzten Teil der Webcast-Serie zu den SAP Success Plans präsentierte Stephanie Lockemann, verantwortlich für das Premium Customer Innovation Service Segment in Mittel- und Osteuropa, das umfassendste Angebot im neuen Portfolio: den Max Success Plan. Während die ersten beiden Webcasts den Gesamtüberblick und den Advanced Plan behandelten, ging es hier um die Frage, was strategische Partnerschaft mit SAP auf höchstem Level konkret bedeutet. Anbei die Zusammenfassung.

 

Was ist der Max Success Plan von SAP? 

Stephanie Lockemann machte einen Punkt, der sich wie ein roter Faden durch den gesamten Vortrag zog: Der Max Success Plan ist kein reines Safeguarding für den Go-Live. Er soll Kunden in eine kontinuierliche Nutzung und Optimierung ihrer Software-Investitionen bringen – über den gesamten Lebenszyklus der Cloud-Subskription hinweg.

Der entscheidende Unterschied zu den bisherigen Premium-Engagement-Angeboten: Kunden mussten bislang drei verschiedene Dienstleistungsengagements mit SAP eingehen. Mit dem Max Success Plan ist alles in einem Modell integriert – Foundational, Advanced und Max sind aufeinander aufgebaut und in einer Subskription enthalten.

 

Was der Max Plan über den Advanced Plan hinaus bietet

Lockemann ging systematisch durch die Elemente, die den Max Plan von den anderen beiden Stufen abheben:

Dedizierter Success Plan Manager

Das Kernstück des Max Plans. Eine namentlich zugeordnete Person – idealerweise regelmäßig vor Ort beim Kunden – die das gesamte SAP-Engagement proaktiv koordiniert. Für bestehende Premium-Engagement-Kunden: Die Rolle entwickelt sich aus dem Technical Quality Manager (TQM) weiter. Was Kunden am TQM schätzen, bleibt erhalten.

Der Success Plan Manager arbeitet eng mit dem Kundenteam zusammen, versteht die sich weiterentwickelnden Bedürfnisse und koordiniert den Zugang zu einem globalen SAP-Expertenpool von rund 27.000 Fachleuten. Er führt Scoping Calls durch, stellt sicher, dass die richtige Person mit dem richtigen Werkzeug zum richtigen Zeitpunkt eingesetzt wird, und passt den Serviceplan kontinuierlich an.

Premium Support SLAs

Eine initiale Reaktionszeit von 15 Minuten für Prio-sehr-hoch-Fälle. Lockemann ordnete realistisch ein: 15 Minuten Reaktionszeit bedeutet nicht 15 Minuten Lösung. Aber gerade während Go-Lives oder in geschäftskritischen Situationen und Bereichen sind diese SLAs entscheidend.

Support Case Management Assistance

Dedizierte Unterstützung bei der Bearbeitung hochprioritärer Support-Fälle, ergänzt durch Reporting und Enablement.

Transformation Advisory Services

Begleitung von der Roadmap bis zur Realisierung mit einem klaren Adoptionsplan – entweder für eine spezifische Lösung oder lösungsübergreifend über End-to-End-Prozesse hinweg. Die Services umfassen Transformationsplanung, Design, Safeguarding, Architekturtransformation und Governance. 

Maßgeschneiderte KI Use Case Adoption

Hier geht der Max Plan deutlich über den Advanced Plan hinaus. Wo Advanced bei der Identifikation und ersten Aktivierung von KI-Use-Cases aufhört, setzt Max zusätzlich bei der Umsetzung an: Befähigung des Center of Excellence, Entwicklung kundenindividueller KI-Lösungen auf der BTP – auch über Standard-Use-Cases hinaus – und kontinuierlicher Betrieb dieser Lösungen.

Lösungsübergreifende Process Improvement Services

Analyse, Verbesserung und Monitoring von End-to-End-Geschäftsprozessen. Dazu gehören Business Transformation Management, Cyber Security, Compliance, Data und Analytics Adoption, Datenqualität und -architektur sowie ein Operations Control Center für die Cloud. 

Premium Services: Was bleibt, was ist neu?

Für bestehende Premium-Engagement-Kunden skizzierte Stephanie Lockemann die folgende Gegenüberstellung:

Was bleibt:

  • Die strategische Partnerschaft mit dedizierten Experten und Enterprise-Architektur-Unterstützung
  • Zugang zum Katalog spezifischer Premium Services
  • Die bewährte Governance-Struktur

Was neu dazukommt:

  • Unbegrenzter Zugang zu Cloud-basierten Leistungen aus allen drei Layers der Success Plans
  • KI-gestützte, eingebettete Tools für kontinuierliches Monitoring und Empfehlungen
  • Aktivierungs- und Optimierungsunterstützung für die Lösung
  • Schnellere Premium-Support-Reaktionszeiten durch verbesserte SLAs 

Vordefinierte Servicepläne: Schneller starten mit Erfahrungswissen

Ein praxisnahes Element, das Lockemann vorstellte: SAP hat vordefinierte Servicepläne für typische Kundenszenarien entwickelt. Diese basieren auf dem Erfahrungsschatz aus zahlreichen Premium-Engagement-Projekten und decken Szenarien ab von der Vorbereitung und Umsetzung von Cloud-Transformationen über einzelne Public-Cloud-Lösungen bis hin zur Nutzung neuester SAP-Innovationen.

Die Pläne können direkt übernommen oder angepasst und erweitert werden. Der Success Plan Manager hilft bei der Auswahl und Individualisierung. Das Ziel: den Start beschleunigen und erleichtern, statt jedes Engagement von Grund auf neu zu planen. 

KI im Max Plan: Von der Guidance zur Umsetzung

Lockemann zeigte anhand einer Swimlane-Darstellung, wie die drei Plan-Stufen beim Thema KI ineinandergreifen:

  • Foundational: Joule ist standardmäßig enthalten, ebenso eingebettete KI-Funktionen in den Lösungen und Lernangebote
  • Advanced: KI zur Beschleunigung der Transformation durch Guidance und Aktivierung neuer Funktionalitäten
  • Max: Umfassende KI-Strategie mit Identifikation und Implementierung von Use Cases – auch über Standard hinaus. Entwicklung von Customer AI auf der BTP, Optimierung und Betrieb eigener KI-Lösungen

Das Besondere dabei ist auch, dass der Success Plan Manager über alle drei Ebenen hinweg orchestriert und für jede Situation das passende Service-Element auswählt – manchmal ein Self-Service-Enablement aus dem Foundational Layer, manchmal einen Aktivierungsservice aus Advanced, manchmal eine tiefe Max-Beratung. 

Abbildung: Was bietet welcher Plan rund um AI?

Abbildung: AI Erweiterungen auf der SAP BTP als Teil des Max Success Plans 

Der Dreiklang des Mehrwerts

Den Wert des Max Success Plans kann man in drei Dimensionen zusammen fassen:

  1. Maximierung des Return on Investment: Das höchste Level an Advisory sichert die Transformation ab und beschleunigt die Wertrealisierung.
  2. Proaktive strategische Partnerschaft: SAP prüft aktiv, welche Cloud-Lösungen im Einsatz sind, identifiziert ungenutztes Potenzial und kommt mit Vorschlägen – nicht erst auf Anfrage.
  3. Kontinuierliche Transformation: Servicepläne sind gezielt entwickelt, um Innovation zu beschleunigen, KI zu aktivieren und das Center of Excellence zu befähigen. 

Aussagen aus dem Webinar

„Es geht nicht mehr nur darum, Ihr Implementierungsprojekt und Ihren Go-Live abzusichern. Wir identifizieren jetzt auch proaktiv Möglichkeiten zur Innovation und zur Optimierung und fördern messbare Ergebnisse über die gesamte Lebensdauer Ihrer SAP-Landschaft."
„Der Success Plan Manager ist wirklich Ihr Trusted Advisor, der dafür verantwortlich ist, dass Sie den gesamten Wert Ihrer SAP-Lösung ausschöpfen können."
„Es geht nicht darum, in jedem Fall einen Max-Service zu positionieren, sondern wirklich einen Serviceplan aufzubauen, der sich aus allen drei Layers bedient."
„15 Minuten initiale Reaktionszeit heißt nicht 15 Minuten Lösung. Aber gerade während Go-Lives sind diese Premium-Support-SLAs entscheidend."
„Der Max Success Plan ist kein reines Safeguarding. Er soll Sie in eine kontinuierliche Nutzung Ihrer Software-Investitionen bringen, sodass Sie das Optimum herausholen können."

Praktische Tipps für die Umsetzung

  1. Vordefinierte Servicepläne als Ausgangspunkt nutzen: SAP hat typische Szenarien bereits durchdacht. Starten Sie nicht bei null, sondern passen Sie einen bestehenden Plan an Ihre Situation an.

  2. Den Success Plan Manager strategisch einbinden: Behandeln Sie ihn nicht als reinen Service-Koordinator, sondern als strategischen Sparringspartner, der Ihre Geschäftsziele kennt und SAP-seitig orchestriert.

  3. Center of Excellence frühzeitig aufbauen: Der Max Plan bietet die Befähigung – aber die interne Verankerung von KI-Kompetenz und Innovationskultur muss vom Unternehmen selbst getragen werden. Der CoE Ansatz hilft dabei.

  4. Die drei Layers bewusst mischen: Nicht jede Fragestellung braucht einen Max-Service. Manchmal reicht ein Self-Service-Enablement oder ein Advanced-Aktivierungsservice. Der Mehrwert liegt in der intelligenten Orchestrierung.

  5. Cyber Security und Datenqualität priorisieren: Stephanie Lockemann betonte diese Themen als zentrale Voraussetzung für sinnvolle KI-Nutzung. Wer hier Lücken hat, sollte früh investieren.

  6. Proaktive Vorschläge einfordern: Der Max Plan verspricht, dass SAP ungenutzte Investitionen identifiziert und Vorschläge macht. Fordern Sie das auch aktiv ein.

Resümee

Der Max Success Plan positioniert sich als das umfassendste Beratungs- und Begleitungsangebot, das SAP bietet. Die Konsolidierung von drei bisher getrennten Engagement-Modellen in ein aufeinander aufbauendes Portfolio vereinfacht die Zusammenarbeit. Mit dem dedizierten Success Plan Manager, den Premium Support SLAs und der Möglichkeit, kundenindividuelle KI-Lösungen zu entwickeln und zu betreiben, richtet sich der Plan an Unternehmen mit komplexen Transformationsszenarien, die SAP nicht nur als Softwarelieferanten, sondern als strategischen Partner verstehen. Sehr relevant fand ich den Punkt zu Cyber Security und Datenqualität als Voraussetzung für KI. Das wird in KI-Diskussionen manchmal übergangen, ist aber in der Praxis eine relevante Blockade. Dass SAP das als expliziten Service-Bestandteil im Max Plan verankert, zeigt den umfassenden Charakter des Max Plans.

Zusätzlich ist am 25.06. ein Vor-Ort Event geplant in Walldorf zu den Success Plänen mit Fokus auf Max – Details finden Sie hier.

Fragen und Antworten zum MAX Success Plan 

Wie viele Servicetage sind im Max Plan enthalten?
Die Cloud-basierten Services im Max Plan sind als Entitlements unbegrenzt abrufbar. Für die Premium Services (Transformation Advisory, KI Use Case Adoption, Cross-Solution Process Improvement) gelten die aus dem Premium Engagement bekannten Rahmenbedingungen.

Wie werden die Leistungen von Advanced und Max voneinander abgegrenzt?
Advanced bietet unbegrenzten Zugang zu Innovations-Guidance, Aktivierungs- und Optimierungsservices sowie KI-gestütztem Monitoring – fokussiert auf einzelne Lösungen. Max ergänzt dies um einen dedizierten Success Plan Manager, lösungsübergreifende End-to-End-Services, maßgeschneiderte KI-Entwicklung, Transformation Advisory und Premium Support SLAs mit 15 Minuten Reaktionszeit. Im Advanced Plan gibt es keine Begrenzung der Service-Anzahl, die einen Wechsel zu Max erzwingen würde.

Wir nutzen heute VPS Services – wie grenzt sich das von den Success Plans ab?
Diese Frage wurde im Webinar live beantwortet. Die Success Plans sind eine Weiterentwicklung und Konsolidierung der bisherigen Service-Angebote. Bestehende Kunden sollten ihren SAP-Ansprechpartner kontaktieren, um die konkrete Transition zu besprechen.

Sind die Success Plans auch für On-Premise-Installationen vorgesehen?
Die Success Plans sind für Cloud-Systeme und solche, die perspektivisch in die Cloud gehen, konzipiert. Für Systeme, die langfristig On-Premise bleiben, bietet SAP weiterhin eine abgewandelte Form des Premium Engagements an.

Gibt es eine Kombination aus Success Plans und On-Premise-Engagement – etwa bei BTP (Cloud) neben On-Premise?
Ja, hybride Konstellationen sind möglich. Für On-Premise bleibt das abgewandelte Premium Engagement bestehen. Für Cloud-Lösungen wie BTP können Success Plans in jeder Stufe – auch Advanced oder Max – gebucht werden.

Wie ist das Zusammenspiel mit SAP-Partnern bei der Implementierung?
SAP tritt mit den Success Plans nicht in Konkurrenz zu Implementierungspartnern. Die Plans liefern komplementär Zugang zu SAP-Expertise – insbesondere zu Best Practices, Standards, Architektur und spezialisiertem Fachwissen. Das Ziel ist Entscheidungstransparenz. Die Implementierung bleibt beim Partner.

Sind die Success Plans verpflichtende Bestandteile der Cloud-Transformation?
Der Foundational Plan ist automatisch in der Cloud-Subskription enthalten. Advanced und Max sind optionale Erweiterungen, die situativ empfohlen, aber nicht verpflichtend sind.

Gibt es eine AI Use Case Library?
SAP bietet im Rahmen der Success Plans verschiedene Services zur Identifikation und Aktivierung von KI-Use-Cases an. Für Details zur Verfügbarkeit einer konkreten Use Case Library empfiehlt sich der Kontakt zum SAP-Ansprechpartner. Ein erster Infopunkt ist auch SAPs Discovery Center und der AI Bereich dort.

Wir haben derzeit ActiveAttention – wie können wir auf den neuen Plan wechseln?
Es gibt Möglichkeiten zum Wechsel. SAP empfiehlt, die Details mit dem zuständigen TQM bzw. SAP-Ansprechpartner zu besprechen.

Wie flexibel kann ich zwischen den Success-Plan-Modellen wechseln?
Ein Wechsel zwischen den Stufen (Upgrade oder Downgrade) richtet sich an der Laufzeit der Lizenzverträge. Für Details zum Timing empfiehlt SAP den direkten Kontakt.

Was ist mit den On-Premise-Systemen – werden diese zukünftig über die Success Plans abgedeckt?
Systeme, die perspektivisch in die Cloud gehen, können über die Success Plans abgedeckt werden. Für langfristig On-Premise betriebene Systeme gibt es weiterhin eine abgewandelte Form des Premium Engagements als Herstellersupport.

Welche Rolle spielt der Partner im Support-Konzept der Success Plans?
Der Implementierungspartner bleibt für die Umsetzung verantwortlich. SAP bietet über die Success Plans komplementären Zugang zu Herstellerexpertise – insbesondere zu Best Practices, Standards und Architektur. Hands-on-Aktivierungen im Advanced und Max Plan werden in Abstimmung mit dem Projektteam und dem Partner durchgeführt.

Werden die Folien und Recordings der drei Sessions zur Verfügung gestellt?
Diese Frage wurde im Chat gestellt. Die Anmeldeseite der Webcast-Serie finden Sie unter: SAP Success Plans Webcast-Serie

entry.title: Re: Cannot add new entries in Define attributes of system...
entry.content:
Good day @yasmine_bennani 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud does not provide the option to add new entries in Define Attributes of System Messages for message control. So, if application area 06, message 136 is not already delivered there, you cannot newly maintain it as a blocking error through SSCUI. In other words, you can only change the message category of existing entries. See KBA 3388968 for more insights.

I suggest you check whether your purchasing process can already be controlled by the logic that controls standard info record, such as fixed supplier scenarios, or whether you need to implement an additional validation in the Custom Logic framework for purchase orders. If neither covers your requirement, then consider submitting an improvement request through Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: New SAP Signavio Value Accelerators: Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
We are excited to announce two new additions to the SAP Signavio Value Accelerator Library:

  • Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

These accelerators bring a harmonized process structure that connects the SAP Reference Business Process Architecture with SAP S/4HANA best practices ready to use out of the box. They are built for everyone working on transformation, running Fit-to-Standard workshops, or who’s simply looking to enrich their Signavio workspace with first process content to get started.

Key highlights include:

  • SAP Reference Business Process Architecture including specific end-to-end variants
  • Out-of-the-box synchronization with SAP LeanIX thanks to renewed structure
  • Mapping to S/4HANA best practice diagrams
  • Dictionary entries to capture information structurally

You can find both accelerators directly in your SAP Signavio workspace by opening the Value Accelerator Library (VAL) and searching for "Process Landscape Design".

We have put together more information and a step-by-step installation and setup guide. Check it out here.

 

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to your BTM contact.

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services and Microsoft Entra ID for Joule
entry.content:
I was able to get Joule in Teams running with MS Entra OIDC Corporate IDP additional to the SAML Corporate IDP. Then I was testing with OIDC Corporate IDP in conditional authentication for the Joule relevant apps but I noticed that in some situations accessing the platform was not possible anymore because of changed assertion attributes. Could be related to MS Entra setup but SNI changed to email instead of UUID (as configured in CIS). I noticed in OIDC MS Entra federated SSO scenario the SAP CIS response is not taken as main and that has a negative impact on SSO assertion attributes. Attribute sensitive platforms do not like that. So I am still using SAML Corporate IDP in application conditional auth. and OIDC registration enabled Joule in MS Teams.

SAML seems to be better for SSO and OIDC is more relevant for app to app.

entry.title: Re: How to Copy Price From PO to Intercompany Sales Order
entry.content: The standard process is SO → PO. This being the case, it is not possible to copy PO price to SO with the standard configuration. A BTP extension can achieve PO → SO price transfer, but only by listening for the PO creation event and then updating the SO pricing condition afterward. At creation time, the SO won’t have the PO price — the synchronization happens post‑PO generation.

entry.title: CSV Validator App Is Now Open Source
entry.content:
From Solution to Community Tool

A few weeks ago, I shared how CSVValidationApp helps solve data quality issues in SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub. The response surprised me. Many have built similar workarounds, spent hours cleaning data manually, or watched perfectly good imports fail because of stray HTML tags.

That's when I felt this shouldn't be "my tool." It should be our tool.

Today, CSV Validator App is officially open source and available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license: github.com/SAP/csv-validator

Why This Matters

I didn't build this tool because I love CSV files (I don't). I built it because I was tired of seeing good work derailed by preventable issues. An HR team prepares a skills library update, exports clean data, imports it into TIH—and suddenly descriptions are broken, special characters are corrupted, and they're troubleshooting instead of enabling their workforce.

It's frustrating. And it's common.

Making this open source isn't about recognition or downloads. It's about honesty: I don't have all the answers. You might be importing recruiting data with different quirks. You might support a region with specific character encoding needs. You might have found a validation rule I missed.

By opening the code, we can solve these problems together—transparently, collaboratively, and faster than any of us could alone.

What You're Getting

This is a complete SAP Fiori application you can run today:

Core Validation:

  • Detects encoding issues (UTF-8 compliance, BOM, null bytes)
  • Validates CSV structure (headers, delimiters, column consistency)
  • Identifies problematic content (HTML tags, entities, control characters, special characters)
  • Checks field lengths and data integrity

Auto-Correction:

  • Strips HTML tags while preserving content
  • Converts HTML entities to UTF-8 characters (  → space, ü → ü)
  • Properly escapes special characters
  • Generates a clean, download-ready corrected file

UI Features:

  • Drag-and-drop file upload
  • Interactive preview of your CSV
  • Filterable table showing issues by row and type
  • Side-by-side view of original vs. corrected content
  • One-click download of the corrected file

Privacy:

  • Everything runs locally in your browser
  • No uploads, no servers, no data leaves your machine

It's built with SAP UI5, follows SAP Fiori standards, and runs via npm. Five minutes from clone to validation.

Getting Started

git clone https://github.com/SAP/csv-validator.git
cd csv-validator
npm install
npm start

Open your browser to the app url, drop in a CSV file, and click Validate. That's it.
You can test by uploading your csv and press validate. Here is a sample screenshot of what you can expect:

 

Where We Go From Here

I have ideas for improvements—support for other SuccessFactors modules, batch processing, direct API integration. But I'm more interested in hearing yours.

If you use this tool and it helps, tell me what's missing. If it doesn't catch an issue it should, open a GitHub issue with an example. If you have a fix or enhancement, submit a pull request. If you just want to share your experience, leave a comment below.

This isn't a finished product. It's a starting point. I'm learning as much from your use cases as you might learn from the code.

An Honest Ask

If you work with SuccessFactors data imports—whether you're an administrator, consultant, or developer—try this tool with your real files. Not a sanitized demo file. Your actual data (with sensitive info removed, of course).

Then tell me:

  • Did it catch issues you would have missed?
  • Did it flag false positives that aren't actually problems?
  • What validation rule would make your life easier?

And if you've built something similar or have ideas for collaboration, let's talk. The goal isn't to centralize everything into this one tool—it's to raise the baseline for data quality across the community.

Resources:

  • GitHub Repository: github.com/SAP/csv-validator
  • Original Blog Post: Bridging Data Quality Gaps in TIH with CSVValidationApp
  • Issues & Feedback: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/issues
  • Contribution Guidelines: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/CONTRIBUTING.md

Data quality shouldn't be a bottleneck. Let's make it a strength—together.

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter Feature: On-Premise Connectivity via SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi @3Osakar ,

I have been facing the same issue and error message. The solution that worked for me:

  • Define user credentials without domain (only user:password)
  • On "Connection" Tab, fill the "Domain" with the domain of the user (for me all lower case worked out fine).

I found it weird that I was not able to see any errors or connection attemps in cloud connector, therefore I thought of an issue between IS and cloud connector, whereas it actually was a simple authentication issue on the samba share. Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: How to implement a 5HP reverse process (inter-company...
entry.content:
Good day @jesseho271 

Thank you for your question.

For the reverse process, the Public Cloud does not support reusing the NBIC advanced intercompany STO and marking the item as a return. Doing that leads to ME392 because the supplier is plant assigned and the system cannot determine a suitable returns delivery type in that path. You should rather use purchase order type NBF2 to process the return through Intercompany Stock Transfer Returns, without manually setting the Return Item indicator. See KBA 2958447 for more insights.

See Note 3192546 on restrictions regarding Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (scope item 5HP) business process.

 

As a next step, make sure you confirm that 2TS is active in your tenant, then create the return in Create Purchase Order-Advanced with NBF2, keep the Return checkbox empty.

 Also, avoid changing document type or delivery type on existing 5HP documents, because that is an unsupported way to manipulate the process.

Here are references for more guidance

3425587 - Unable to access the SSCUI ID 103426 - SAP for Me

3642372 - Restriction for Changes to Document and Delivery Types in Advanced Intercompany Stock Transport Orders (5HP) - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP TM: Report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR changes addresses of locations to outdated addresses
entry.content:
Hi,

In the Business Partner master data of a ship-to party, two addresses are maintained in the address overview: One old address and one currently valid address. The location corresponding to this ship-to party still contains the old address. Therefore, we used report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR, which initially updated the location address from the old one to the new one. So far, so good.

However, the report does not seem to apply any business logic when selecting the address. It simply switches the address number if a different one exists. As a result, it may also overwrite a correct (current) address with an outdated one when executed again. This can lead to situations where a location contains an address that is no longer used by the Business Partner. From a business perspective, the expected behavior would be that the report determines the currently valid address of the Business Partner and updates the location accordingly.

Is there any alternative to report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR to keep location addresses in sync and up to date with the Business Partner?

entry.title: How can you keep industrial systems consistent at scale?
entry.content:
As a reliability engineer, you rely on industrial systems staying structured and predictable.
In reality, structures can drift over time, especially when changes are made across multiple systems.

In SAP Asset Performance Management (APM), centrally defined templates help prevent this. They act as a single source of truth for system structure and hierarchy, ensuring what you see reflects an approved, governed state.

This blog shows how to update templates and synchronize linked systems. You can follow along with the accompanying video for a step-by-step demo.

Update and Synchronize Template-Based Industrial Systems | SAP Asset Performance Management

Why Templates Matter for Reliability Engineers

Industrial systems in SAP APM derive from templates that define standard structures, including active and intentionally deactivated elements. This lets you focus analysis and maintenance on relevant assets only, avoiding irrelevant parts.

Key benefits:

  • Clear linkage: Every template-based system shows its template connection and follows its definition
  • Scalable management: Filter and sort elements by activation status to quickly spot active, inactive, or excluded parts
  • Bulk operations: Select multiple elements for activation/deactivation in one action, or use Excel upload for mass updates (add an "Activate Element" column for imports)

Templates enable controlled change management...update once, push everywhere, delivering governance at scale: consistent structures, intentional changes, no drift over time, reduced manual effort, and fewer errors across large landscapes.

Step-by-Step: Updating and Pushing Template Changes

1. Access the Template

Open the Manage Industrial System Templates app. Select a released template. Check Template Usage Count to view linked industrial systems and their versions.

2. Enter Revision Mode

Choose Change Status > Under Revision to unlock editing. Select Edit - fields become editable.

3. Modify Structure

Navigate to Structure (graph view shows relationships clearly). Add, remove, or adjust elements:

  • Click + to add (e.g., subordinate air conditioning element to an air conditioning station)
  • Fill description, set object class code, and Create
  • Cross-reference or re-parent as needed

4. Finalize and Release

Save changes. Set Change Status > Released. Push Changes button appears.

5. Push to Linked Systems

Dialog lists affected systems. Select targets (e.g., one linked system):

  • Created/Reopened systems update automatically
  • Released systems update and re-release

Trigger Push Changes to create a background job.

6. Monitor and Verify

Track via Job List. Once complete:

  • Return to Manage Industrial Systems app
  • Confirm structure matches updated template (e.g., new substation appears)
  • Sync Status verifies synchronization

Flexible System-Level Adjustments

Templates govern the standard, but adapt per system:

  • Deactivate elements (e.g., sub-station out of service/not commissioned) without altering the template
  • Reactivate anytime to reflect operational state - no manual restructuring needed

Governance Benefits: Single Source of Truth in Action

This workflow eliminates error-prone manual tweaks, ensuring consistency, completeness, and correctness across systems. Like a true SSOT, templates standardize definitions, propagate changes reliably, and build trust—no more conflicting structures or "which version is right?" debates.

Reliability engineers gain confidence in governed, synchronized assets, supporting reliable analytics and maintenance at enterprise scale.

Ready to implement? Follow the video demo in the APM system and experience governance without the hassle.

Have you experienced challenges with maintaining consistent system structures across multiple industrial assets before implementing template-based governance? Share in the comments below!

Don’t forget to submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP Asset Performance Management! Furthermore, explore our SAP Asset Performance Management Topic Page and follow the tag SAP Asset Performance Management to not miss out on more content to come! @SonamKinger @omprakash_balakrishnan2 

SAP Asset Performance Management

entry.title: Re: SLD DB Export/ Import issue
entry.content:
Hi sbadawi,

If you are using SAP Business One version for SAP HANA, a safer workaround is to avoid relying on manual export/import actions in SLD for backups.

You can implement a scripted export based on the SAP HANA export commands described in the official documentation check note https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/2134959 , and then schedule it using a cron job.

This way, the export process becomes automated and reduces the risk of accidental misuse, such as selecting import instead of export and overwriting the database with a previous backup.

To my knowledge, if there is no option in SLD to restrict a user to the export operation only, then automation through script is a practical and safer alternative.

entry.title: Q1/2026 Product Highlights - SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Welcome to another quarterly recap of the latest released features in SAP Integration Suite! The new capabilities in Q1/2026 focus on better operations and smoother eventing for integration practitioners who build, run, and troubleshoot integrations every day.  Enjoy the highlights and check the linked docs and demos for how-to details and the road map for the full list.

Enhanced Edition is here

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition helps move AI and agentic AI from pilots to production by providing governed, real-time connectivity, low-code developer tools, and AI-driven operations (Text-to-Flow, Script Optimization, API Anomaly Detection, API Traffic Prediction, plus connectors, event mesh, Document AI, alerting, and transport management). It reduces integration debt, enables secure agent actions across cloud and on-prem systems, and creates reusable building blocks for faster, repeatable AI value.

Accelerate your AI initiatives and make them enterprise-ready, get more details in the SAP Discovery Center.

Event-driven Integration

Discovery and publication of advanced event mesh events in the Developer Hub

Content administrators can discover and publish Advanced Event Mesh events directly in the Developer Hub, enabling events to be exposed as event products and making it easier for application developers to search and find relevant events. To use it, you create a destination to connect SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to the Developer Hub; once connected, the business system appears in the Developer Hub for discovery and publication.

For more detailed instructions, check the documentation: Discover and Publish Events from Advanced Event Mesh in Developer Hub.

 

Migration support from SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This update provides technical and process-oriented support to help customers migrate from SAP Event Mesh (default plan) to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, enabling organizations to move their event-driven architectures to a more powerful, feature-rich platform. The guide targets customers already using the SAP Event Mesh default plan and outlines steps to expand their event-based architecture. Read the guide: Migration From SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh.

Process Integration

Valkey support for Edge Integration Cell

The Edge Integration Cell capability in SAP Integration Suite now supports Valkey as an alternative datastore option alongside Redis and HANA DB. This option is available as selectable installation supporting versions 8.0-8.2 and allows administrators to choose Valkey for deployments helping reduce total cost of ownership.

For the steps and prerequesites, see: Deploy the Edge Integration Cell Solution

Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM

Users can monitor message processing from Edge Integration Cell using a push-based approach to send logs to SAP Focused Run (FRUN) and SAP Cloud ALM (CALM). This enables centralized monitoring of multiple Edge Integration Cell runtimes within a single application, lets you configure alerts based on message processing log status, and supports monitoring of Edge Integration Cell message processing logs in SAP Focused Run even during offline scenarios.

See Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM for more information.

Message queue management: extension and filtering of scenario details in the message queue monitor

The message queue monitor now displays scenario-specific attributes, such as Sender, Receiver, Application Message Type, Application Message ID, and Correlation ID, which can be enabled in the Messages table settings and used as filter criteria.

This feature lets you narrow messages more efficiently and quickly identify problematic entries with immediate context, reducing mean time to resolution by allowing direct filtering to relevant messages, enabling more precise troubleshooting and root-cause analysis, cutting operational noise by surfacing only the messages that matter, improving auditing and compliance with visible searchable metadata, and simplifying collaboration and handover between operations, support, and development teams. Get started: Managing Messages Stored in Queues

Support for parameterized mappings for the adapter type

The import options from SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration have been extended to support parameters in message mappings for the adapter category, enabling you to import PI/PO message mappings that include adapter-type parameters; migration tooling and standalone message mapping import now accept mapping objects with the Adapter Type parameter category of type Import, which preserves adapter-specific parameterization, reduces manual adjustments and rework during migration, improves fidelity of migrated mappings, and accelerates onboarding and troubleshooting by maintaining runtime behavior tied to adapter parameters. See documentation: Import a Message Mapping from Enterprise Services Repository (ESR)

Connectivity Updates

Customer-managed cloud connectors

The Edge Integration Cell runtime now supports using a customer-managed external SAP Cloud Connector instead of the built-in, SAP-managed connector, allowing organizations to deploy the connector in a network zone that best fits their infrastructure and security requirements. This feature adds deployment flexibility, improves security controls, enables additional traffic inspection capabilities, and helps meet infrastructure and compliance needs. See configuration steps: Configure an External Cloud Connector for Edge Integration Cell

Additionally, users can now add and initialize an Edge Node with an external Cloud Connector through Edge Lifecycle Management. See how: Initializing an Edge Node with External Cloud Connector

Latest adapters

The list of released adapter in Q1/2026 includes:

  • BambooHR adapter: Connect to BambooHR to access APIs related to employee, organizational, time off data etc.
  • Zendesk adapter: Offers a comprehensive set of Ticketing CRUD operations for various entities such as Tickets, Attachments, Requests, Tags, etc. The adapter is designed to receive and process data from an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite and facilitate communication with Zendesk application (external) using REST-based APIs.
  • Microsoft 365 Outlook adapter: Enables seamless integration between Microsoft Outlook and SAP Integration Suite by improving communication and automating workflows between the two platforms. Available as a Sender and Receiver adapter. 🎥 Watch the demo. 
  • OFTP2 adapter: Use the Odette File Transfer Protocol (OFTP) adapter to exchange EDI and business files with partners on your Edge Integration Cell.
  • NFS File adapter: Enables connectivity to NFS server facilitating read and write operations for files on your Edge Integration Cell.  🎥 Watch the demo.

Updated data center availability

The new data center availability options for SAP Integration Suite include: Australia (Sydney) on SAP infrastructure, Japan (Tokyo) and USA on an SAP-hosted infrastructure, and the United Kingdom through Microsoft Azure.

---

Learn more

Want a sneak peek into what else is in store for SAP Integration Suite in 2026? Watch this walkthrough:

 What are the key innovation you're looking forward to in 2026? Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: AI‑Driven Procurement Analytics with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, and Joule
entry.content:
AI‑Driven Procurement Analytics with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, and Joule

Procurement teams are under pressure to go beyond static reports and deliver proactive, data‑driven recommendations. SAP now offers an end‑to‑end Business AI stack that can do exactly that  from embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA to AI‑assisted insights in SAP Analytics Cloud and generative AI with Joule.

This post explains how SAP Business AI supports procurement analytics across the SAP portfolio and how customers can combine native capabilities with external AI where required  fully aligned with SAP’s official strategy and product documentation.

SAP Business AI in a Procurement Context

SAP Business AI is SAP’s integrated suite of AI capabilities delivered across applications and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). It is designed to work on reliable business data, follow industry and regulatory standards, and be embedded directly into end‑to‑end processes such as source‑to‑pay.

According to SAP’s Business AI positioning:

  • SAP offers “AI and agents that actually understand all your business processes and data,” with a “single AI interface that seamlessly integrates data and workflows across your SAP and non‑SAP applications.” Also refer to SAP Business AI Roadmaps 
  • Customers can “drive efficiency across every business function with access to over 230 AI-powered scenarios expanding to 400 by the end of 2025,” including finance, supply chain, and procurement use cases.
  • SAP BTP provides AI foundation services and tools to build, run, and integrate advanced AI solutions and agents at scale.

For procurement, these capabilities manifest in three main layers:

  1. Embedded analytics and AI inside SAP S/4HANA and SAP’s spend‑management solutions.
  2. AI‑assisted analytics and planning in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  3. Generative AI and copilots such as Joule, running across the portfolio.

Embedded Analytics and AI in SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes powerful embedded analytics with SAP Fiori analytical apps, CDS‑based data models, and built‑in AI features. SAP describes these as “AI‑driven interactions” that help users improve productivity and obtain quick insights directly in the operational system.

Key characteristics for procurement:

  • Real‑time visibility on live transactional data from purchasing documents, suppliers, and materials.
  • SAP Fiori analytical apps and KPI tiles for purchasing performance, open items, supplier reliability, and more, which are part of the standard S/4HANA content.
  • Integration into Fiori elements and analytical floor plans that support drill‑down, filtering, and smart visualizations.

These capabilities are ideal for operational and tactical decisions (for example, monitoring overdue POs or supplier delivery performance) and provide a governed, SAP‑delivered starting point for procurement analytics. They can be combined with additional AI services via SAP BTP if customers need more advanced data science workloads.

Official reference: “Enabling new, AI-driven interactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Fiori elements” (SAP asset).

SAP Analytics Cloud: AI‑Assisted Analytics and Planning

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is SAP’s strategic solution for analytics and planning and is a central pillar of SAP Business AI for analytics scenarios.

The SAC product page describes how customers can:

  • “Embrace the power of generative AI to automate reporting, discover hidden insights, and develop business plans with Joule copilot.”
  • “Use AI to automatically reveal relationships in data, hidden patterns, and outliers, and to predict potential outcomes and forecasts.”
  • Bring together data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP HANA, SAP Datasphere, and other sources for BI, planning, and predictive analysis in a single experience.

The “Artificial Intelligence for Planning & Analytics” and AI‑assisted features guides describe specific SAC capabilities for business users:

  • Smart Insights, Smart Discovery, Smart Predict  ML‑driven features that help users understand key influencers, detect patterns, and generate predictive forecasts.
  • Search to Insight / Just Ask  natural language query interfaces that allow users to ask questions in plain language and receive visualizations or answers.
  • AI‑assisted features  such as AI‑generated calculations, chart summaries, and explanations integrated into core workflows.

These capabilities are directly integrated into the user interface and can be enabled and governed centrally by administrators. For procurement, this means that category managers or buyers can:

  • Explore spend patterns and outliers using Smart Discovery.
  • Ask natural language questions about spend, supplier performance, or forecasted volumes using Just Ask.
  • Build plans and scenarios with AI‑supported forecasting and explanation features.

Joule: SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

Joule is SAP’s generative AI copilot and is a key element of SAP Business AI. SAP describes Joule as a “single AI interface” that operates across SAP solutions, helping users get more done faster by understanding both business data and processes. For detailed information about Joule capabilities and setup, visit the Joule Help Portal and explore the Joule Community Hub for implementation guidance and best practices.

In analytics and planning, the SAC product page states that customers can use Joule to “automate reporting, discover hidden insights, and develop business plans” by interacting with analytics content using natural language. On the Business AI community pages, SAP highlights that Joule can complete navigational and transactional tasks significantly faster for users across different lines of business.

From a procurement‑analytics perspective, Joule enables scenarios such as:

  • Conversational questions on procurement KPIs (for example, spend by category, supplier performance trends, or contract utilization) with answers grounded in SAC models and S/4HANA data.
  • Narrative summaries of charts, dashboards, and plans, helping stakeholders quickly understand the drivers behind spend or forecast changes.
  • Assistance in creating or adapting analytic content and planning models more efficiently.
  • For a complete list of procurement use cases and implementation details, see the Joule Capabilities Guide for Sourcing and Procurement.

SAP Business AI for Procurement: Spend and Supplier Intelligence

SAP describes “AI in procurement” and “AI for procurement” as a set of Business AI capabilities embedded across SAP’s spend‑management portfolio. According to SAP, these solutions help procurement organizations to:

  • Analyse and optimize spend  AI‑driven spend analysis to identify spending patterns, uncover savings opportunities, and support category strategy decisions.
  • Enhance sourcing and supplier management  evaluate supplier performance, risk, and sustainability and recommend suitable suppliers based on a combination of internal and external data.
  • Improve compliance and risk control  automatically check purchases against contracts and policies, and detect anomalies or suspicious patterns.
  • Automate document processing  use document AI to extract, classify, and validate information from invoices, orders, and contracts, reducing manual effort and errors.

Examples from SAP’s official resources include:

  • “AI in procurement: A complete guide,” which outlines how AI supports spend analysis, sourcing, contract compliance, and supplier risk management.
  • “Discover Advanced AI Procurement Solutions | SAP Business AI,” which summarizes AI‑based spend analytics, supplier insights, and process automation capabilities within SAP’s spend‑management offerings.
  • SAP Document AI, an end‑to‑end intelligent document processing solution that can be used to automate document‑heavy procurement processes (for example, invoice processing).

These domain‑specific AI capabilities provide out‑of‑the‑box value for procurement organizations and are designed to work together with embedded analytics and SAC.

Extending with SAP BTP and AI Foundation

For customers with requirements that go beyond standard content and embedded AI, SAP BTP provides the foundation to build and operate custom AI scenarios.

Key elements include:

  • SAP AI Foundation and AI services  a portfolio of services on SAP BTP (for example, SAP AI Core) to develop, deploy, and manage AI applications.
  • AI units and Business AI Catalog  a consumption‑based model to access SAP Business AI services consistently across SAP solutions, along with a catalog that lists available AI capabilities and their technical and commercial details.
  • Integration with SAP and non‑SAP data  secure connectivity and APIs to combine SAP data with external data sources and expose results back to S/4HANA, SAC, or other applications.

The SAP Business AI onboarding resources describe how organizations can govern AI usage and track AI unit consumption across services. This provides a controlled way to integrate external AI technologies while keeping SAP BTP as the core platform for AI operations and lifecycle management.

Putting It Together: A Reference Architecture for Procurement Analytics

Based on SAP’s official positioning, a typical architecture for AI‑driven procurement analytics with SAP Business AI can look like this:

  1. Operational analytics in SAP S/4HANA
    • Use embedded analytics and AI‑driven Fiori apps on purchasing documents, suppliers, and materials to monitor day‑to‑day performance on live data.
  2. Advanced analytics and planning in SAP Analytics Cloud
    • Connect to S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and other sources to perform AI‑assisted analysis (Smart Insights, Smart Discovery, Just Ask) and planning on procurement data.
  3. Generative AI with Joule
    • Enable Joule as a copilot across SAC and other SAP cloud solutions so buyers and category managers can ask natural‑language questions, generate narratives, and automate routine analytics tasks.
  4. Procurement‑specific Business AI
    • Activate AI capabilities within SAP’s spend‑management solutions (for example, AI‑driven spend analysis, supplier risk, document AI) to address domain‑specific scenarios.
  5. Extensions on SAP BTP
    • Use SAP BTP and AI Foundation to develop custom AI models, agents, or integrations where customers want to combine SAP data with external AI services or build very specialized use cases, managed via AI units and the Business AI Catalog.

This architecture keeps SAP as the system of record and governance layer while allowing organizations to add external AI techniques where helpful, always mediated through SAP BTP and SAP Business AI.

How to Get Started

Customers who want to advance their procurement analytics with SAP Business AI can take the following steps, fully aligned with SAP’s guidance:

  • Review the SAP Business AI white paper and product page to understand SAP’s overall AI strategy and how it applies to their landscape.
  • Explore the Business AI Catalog and Business AI use‑case library to identify relevant procurement scenarios and estimate AI unit consumption.
  • Activate and adopt embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA and AI‑assisted features in SAP Analytics Cloud, including Just Ask and Smart features, for procurement‑related models.
  • Work with their SAP account team and Customer Engagement Executive to clarify entitlements, AI unit model, and roadmap for Joule and procurement‑specific AI services in their region and product portfolio.

With this approach, procurement organizations can move step‑by‑step from static reporting to AI‑assisted insights and, ultimately, to generative AI copilots embedded in their daily workflows  all within the boundaries of SAP’s officially supported platforms and services.

Example : ME2M‑Style Open PO Analysis

Many procurement teams, including yours, routinely analyse outputs from classic reports such as ME2M (open purchase orders) to understand purchasing behaviour, policy exceptions, open commitments, and other operational patterns. SAP’s own guidance on procurement analytics and Business AI maps well to this type of use case.

At a high level, the ME2M‑style analysis could be re‑implemented using SAP’s native layers as follows:

Step / question

SAP capability to use

What it does for ME2M‑type data

“Can we analyse open PO data without exporting to external tools?”

S/4HANA Procurement Analytics and Embedded Analytics (CDS‑based virtual data model and Fiori analytical apps)

Provides real‑time views on purchasing documents and open items using analytical floor plans and overview pages, without data extracts.

“Can we see patterns, anomalies, and drivers in this data?”

SAP Analytics Cloud Smart Discovery, Smart Insights, and predictive features on a ME2M‑equivalent data model

Automatically surfaces key drivers, outliers, and trends in open PO and commitment data modelled from S/4HANA into SAC.

“Can users ask questions in natural language instead of building queries?”

SAP Analytics Cloud “Just Ask” / Search to Insight and Joule copilot

Allows buyers and analysts to ask questions about open POs (for example, by supplier, material, or plant) and get governed, visual answers.

“Can we connect this to more advanced or custom AI if needed?”

SAP BTP AI services and SAP Business AI for Procurement

Enables domain‑specific AI (spend/supplier insights) and side‑by‑side custom models that work on curated procurement data sets.

Concretely, S/4HANA Cloud procurement analytics already provides embedded analytical content (such as the Procurement Overview page, open purchase order‑related tiles, and supplier evaluation) that runs on live procurement data without extraction. This gives you a governed starting point for open PO visibility that covers much of what users currently pull from ME2M.

You can then expose the relevant CDS‑based procurement data into SAP Datasphere or directly into SAP Analytics Cloud and apply AI‑assisted features there. Using Smart Discovery and Smart Insights, teams can look for patterns such as recurring min–max policy breaches, unusual lead times, or atypical price or quantity behaviour in the open PO population.

Finally, with Joule and “Just Ask” connected to SAP Analytics Cloud, buyers can ask natural‑language questions such as “Show me open PO value by supplier with overdue items” or “Which materials have the highest number of min–max violations this month?” and receive answers grounded in the SAC models built on S/4HANA data. For more advanced needs, SAP Business AI for Procurement and SAP BTP‑based AI services provide additional options to detect risk patterns and optimize spend, still using SAP as the system of record.

Information Accuracy

  • Information presented reflects publicly available SAP documentation as of April 2026 and may not reflect the most current product capabilities or availability.
  • Readers should verify current product features, licensing requirements, and regional availability through official SAP channels before making implementation decisions.

entry.title: Re: The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 do...
entry.content: Check SAP note 3516829

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - delivery cretion with bom and deliver group
entry.content: The proportional reduction of BOM subitems happens because of delivery group logic. If you want to adjust only one subitem’s quantity, you must avoid using delivery groups. In Public Cloud, there’s no configuration to change this behavior; the only workaround is to drop the delivery group assignment for BOMs where independent adjustment is required.

entry.title: Not able to access change WO anymore with 2 or 3 clicks
entry.content:
we a have group of front line staff on platforms not able to access change WO via the routes they were able to a few weeks ago

  • Smart link “change WO”missing in enterprise search
  • Route clicking WO header and then Edit throws up an error
  • What seems to work is a 3 clicks approach. From search > display WO > Edit

is this an authorization action we can tackle ourselves or if this is a SAP bug in the standard roles 

Search - S/4HANA Cloud quick link. many colleages do not see "change maintenance order" anymore. 

specific example

 

 

 

The route which DOES work for endusers is via Enterprise Search > Display workorder > Edit. Apparently, that’s transaction WDA0273. So root cause seems to be with WDA0272.

entry.title: SAP Build Process automation: Capturing SAP GUI 8.00 Screen fails
entry.content:

The screen could not be captured. Relaunch the recorder.
I have already restarted the recorder (and the laptop) multiple time without any change.

File Name:          saplogon.exe 
File Version:      8000.1.17.1161 
Build:                  2296049
Patch Level:      17

I use a BTP trial account. I "only" have Desktop Agent version 3.32.58 available, not sure if that causes issues.
I would appreciate any help.

 

Thanks in advance
Joachim

entry.title: Re: SBPA Destination Error: Destination not found.
entry.content:
Hi @Abderrahamane_Kermiche ,

To trigger a workflow in SAP Build Process Automation, a destination needs to be configured in SAP BTP with the name sap_process_automation_service_user_access.

I have created a blog post explaining this setup in detail—you can refer to it here:
Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from... - SAP Community

I have also attached the destination configuration file, which you can import into your SAP BTP account.
Note: Please make sure to update the following values after import:

  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • Token URL

(You can find these details in the service key created for your SAP Build Process Automation instance.)
Feel free to reach out if you face any issues.

Best regards,
Abirami T

entry.title: Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile

Introduction

With recent releases and the move to the Latest People Profile, many existing deep links in SAP SuccessFactors that navigate to a specific employee section or portlet in the legacy profile may no longer work as expected or may not work at all.

Previously, navigation to a specific employee was commonly done using the User ID (users-sys-id). With the latest People Profile, navigation is now based on Assignment UUID.

This is further detailed in the SAP documentation regarding deep links for profile categories and cards: Deep Links to Full Profile Categories and Cards 

Scenario

URL Format

Go to a specific category for a specific user

https://<app-server-domain>/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=<assignment UUID>&categoryId=<category Id>

This example links to a user's Job Data category:

Example

https://www.example.com/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=B2F70B823E3B4AD08091F6D1FBB74313&categoryId=jobData

If the <assignment UUID> is not specified in the URL, the deep link defaults to the signed-in user's profile rather than the intended employee's profile.

In this blog, we focus on how to navigate to a specific employee using the Assignment UUID, which is not readily available in SAP SuccessFactors and must be fetched using Integration Center for deep links.

Understanding User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

SAP SuccessFactors uses multiple identifiers to represent a person and employment data in the system.

A full overview of the available Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors is available here: Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

Assignment UUID

Field

Description

Assignment UUID

Assignment UUIDs are 32 characters and use all uppercase letters (for example: FDF7921CD3C242F59963B30E8895D04A). They are system-generated identifiers and are used in URLs and APIs without exposing personal employee data.

Can it be changed?

No

Can it be used for SSO?

No

Can it be used for integration?

Yes

Where is it displayed on People Profile?

NA

Making Assignment UUID Available for Dynamic Deep Links

The goal is to use the Assignment UUID as a dynamic token in deep links that navigate directly to an employee profile in SAP SuccessFactors.

To achieve this, the Assignment UUID must first be fetched and made available for reuse in templates or notifications. We can implement a custom solution using the Metadata Framework (MDF) and Integration Center.

Custom MDF to Store Assignment UUID

A custom MDF object is created to store the Assignment UUID. This allows the value to be reused across different use cases.

Fields include:

  • User ID
  • Assignment UUID

View of the Custom MDF object definition in Configure Object Definitions.

Integration Center Job to Populate MDF

An Integration Center job is used to fetch the Assignment UUID and populate the custom MDF object. This job should be configured to run daily to ensure that newly hired employees are included.

Integration Center field mapping, Assignment UUID mapped to the custom MDF object field.

After the Integration Center job execution, the MDF is successfully populated, as shown below. Each record now contains the corresponding Assignment UUID, ready for use in dynamic deep links.

"Manage Data" view of the custom MDF object showing records successfully populated with User IDs and their corresponding Assignment UUIDs.

Assignment UUID: 249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA

Use Assignment UUID as a Dynamic Token in Deep Links

Once stored, the Assignment UUID can be used as a dynamic token in a URL in Document Generation via rule mapping, and when the notification is generated, the token is replaced dynamically.

Example deep link:

https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=[[AssignmentUUID]]&categoryId=jobData

Document Generation template displaying the alert message, with the clickable link and the [[AssignmentUUID]] token shown separately to illustrate dynamic value population.

Document Generation template editor displaying the [[AssignmentUUID]] token embedded within the URL link configuration.

Document Mapping Rule (Lookup Function)

Create a rule with a Document Mapping scenario to retrieve the Assignment UUID from MDF using a lookup function.

Business Rule using a Lookup() function to fetch the Assignment UUID from the custom MDF object..

Reference Rule in Document Template Mapping

Document Generation Mapping screen where the [[AssignmentUUID]] token is assigned to the Business Rule

Notifications with Assignment UUID Deep Links

The notification now generates a dynamic link with the Assignment UUID and directs the user to the corresponding employee profile in the Latest People Profile.

The email notification: 

Email notification displaying the clickable deep link populated with the dynamic Assignment UUID.

The Link:
https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA&categoryId=jobData

Successful Navigation to Employee Profile

Employee profile Job Data section displayed after clicking the deep link, confirming successful navigation using the dynamic Assignment UUID.

Summary 

This blog explained how to fetch the Assignment UUID and use it in URL links to navigate to a specific employee profile, category, or card in the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors. Since this identifier is not directly available in the system, a custom solution is implemented to retrieve and maintain it for dynamic use.

The solution is implemented as follows:

  • The Assignment UUID is retrieved using an Integration Center job and stored in a custom MDF object
  • A business rule with a lookup function dynamically fetches the value
  • The Assignment UUID is then used as a token and embedded into deep link URLs in the document generation template

Notes & Key Considerations

  • Job Frequency: Ensure your Integration Center job runs frequently enough to account for new hires.
  • URL Encoding: When building URLs, ensure there are no trailing spaces or characters that could break the string.
